Data Management / POV files
sole interface to the asset DB — no
module touches raw data. Role-filtered
views via patterns DB + Learning Engine:
• viewConstructModule (observe)
• controlConstructModule (actuate)
• adminConstructModule (coordinate)
GridWideEye builds PoV frames.

Federation & autonomy spectrum (oft)
ioc.cc independent, supports central cmd
ioc.sc fully autonomous cell
ioc.coc member of cooperating neighbors
ioc.fm sub-federation member
Cells merge / federate under a scout-master.

div.div:
Security in the protocol
every inter-module message carries a
ga-authenticationkey, validated by a
ga-GateKeeper before any state machine
activates or data is released.
gAVA standardizes the message schema
so all modules speak one language.

Or is it really a local cell 1 transfer itself to cell 2 over local network without cloud involvement?

div.div: CaCSM produces an abstract intent, CaCSM does not have ORACS. But, its supposed to have at list the inventary of devices (what worked in the past), right? Otherwise it will be impossible to reconcile with the Logistician and Asset Portfolio Manager ORACS. What happends when the history is changed? Like new devices where added? What worked on the past might drift from what will work now. Continuous learning?

div.font: 1. No optimality ideas. Learning yes, but no optimality.
2. A giant adaptable lookup table: what worked on the past, lets solve it again in the same way.

3. CaCSM is not a reasoning engine, is a match engine. In this sense, the system will not be able to solve situations that never saw before. So, we will have self-healing only on previously healed conditions.
4. Lack of generalization over the learningWorks only in the network that was learned"

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(240, 163, 10), rgb(154, 88, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(189, 112, 0), rgb(193, 127, 31));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 158px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1550px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ededed); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Can the logistician rely on virtual sensing to replace a missing sensor? How to determine the realiability of the stimated state?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(240, 163, 10), rgb(154, 88, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(189, 112, 0), rgb(193, 127, 31));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 158px; height: 1px; padding-top: 140px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
1. Does the virtual sensing module need to start from zero and stimate state for any grid?
2. Does the virtual sensing module need to be decentralized? Or is it executed on the "cloud" i.e., centralized?
3. Cells can run autonomously while "disconnected" from central control. Do they need virtual sensing capabilities? Will that make the virtual sensing module distributed as well?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(240, 163, 10), rgb(154, 88, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(189, 112, 0), rgb(193, 127, 31));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 158px; height: 1px; padding-top: 865px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ededed); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Several modules talk about "simulating" the effects of the decisions/actions. However, that simulation needs an initial state of the system. Is that initial state of the system provided by virtual sensing? If so, how the uncertainity of that initial state is handled?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(240, 163, 10), rgb(154, 88, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(189, 112, 0), rgb(193, 127, 31));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 158px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1670px; margin-left: 1px;

div.div: How do cells define who is the scout-master? What is the scope of "control" of the scout-master?
